content.title: XG DM Device Migration Firmware
content.short_description: A Python-based device migration firmware system for XG devices that enables communication between Raspberry Pi and NB-IoT modules via UART communic...
content.description: A Python-based device migration firmware system for XG devices that enables communication between Raspberry Pi and NB-IoT modules via UART communication. The system handles device data collection, WebSocket communication, HTTP server posting, and device management with multiprocessing capabilities for real-time device monitoring and control.
content.role: I was the primary developer for the XG DM Device Migration firmware, responsible for implementing the complete communication system. I developed the main application with WebSocket communication, HTTP server posting functionality, and UART communication protocols. I created the UartController class with singleton pattern for serial communication, implemented device data handling with structured objects and arrays, added error handling with try-catch blocks, and managed the entire development workflow with 25+ commits. I also set up the Python virtual environment, managed dependencies, and cleaned up the codebase for production readiness.

achievements: Successfully established communication between Raspberry Pi and NB-IoT modules
Implemented real-time device data collection and server posting
Created robust error handling and device management system
Achieved stable UART communication with proper configuration
Established multiprocessing architecture for concurrent device operations
Delivered production-ready firmware with proper dependency management
